title: Listening - Part 1
description: You are going to read a magazine article about an intellectual process known as critical thinking. For questions 31 – 36, choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which fits best according to the text. Mark your answers on the separate answer sheet.
instructions: You will hear three different extracts. For questions 1–6, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear. There are two questions for each extract.

extracts.context: You hear two friends discussing a new premium music streaming service.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: So, did you notice the difference with the 'Hi-Fi' audio? || Man: [1]I found the mobile app to be quite clunky and slow compared to the standard version[/1], to be honest. It took forever to load my playlists. || Woman: I see what you mean. It’s a bit resource-heavy. || Man: [2]What I really liked, though, was the depth of the sound on my home speakers[/2]. You can really hear the individual instruments in a way you can't with compressed files. || Woman: I agree. The audio fidelity is definitely a step up. || Man: Exactly. It’s a great service for audiophiles, as long as you have a fast enough internet connection to handle the data.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: So, did you notice the difference with the 'Hi-Fi' audio?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I found the mobile app to be quite clunky and slow compared to the standard version, to be honest. It took forever to load my playlists.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I see what you mean. It’s a bit resource-heavy.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: What I really liked, though, was the depth of the sound on my home speakers. You can really hear the individual instruments in a way you can't with compressed files.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I agree. The audio fidelity is definitely a step up.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Exactly. It’s a great service, as long as you have a fast enough internet connection to handle the data.

questions.question: What is the man's main criticism of the service?

options.A: the high cost of the monthly subscription
options.B: the poor performance of the mobile software
options.C: the limited variety of the music library

questions.explanation: He finds the mobile app 'clunky and slow' compared to the standard version [1].

questions.question: Both speakers admired the service's
questions.explanation: They both praise the 'depth of the sound' and the 'audio fidelity' [2].

extracts.context: You hear two colleagues talking about a change in the company's travel policy.
extracts.audio_script: Man: So, no more business class for flights under six hours. What’s your take? || Woman: [3]I suspect the finance team is just trying to hit their quarterly targets rather than thinking about employee fatigue[/3]. || Man: It is a bit of a blow. Arriving at a meeting after a budget flight is never ideal. || Woman: [4]I’ve noticed that people are already starting to delay important trips because they don't want to travel in economy[/4], which could hurt our client relationships. || Man: I agree. It’s a very short-sighted move for a global company. || Woman: Exactly. If they want us to be at our best, they should provide us with the right conditions to do our jobs properly.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: So, no more business class for flights under six hours. What’s your take?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I suspect the finance team is just trying to hit their quarterly targets rather than thinking about employee fatigue.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: It is a bit of a blow. Arriving at a meeting after a budget flight is never ideal.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I’ve noticed that people are already starting to delay important trips because they don't want to travel in economy, which could hurt our client relationships.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It’s a very short-sighted move for a global company.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. If they want us to be at our best, they should provide us with the right conditions to do our jobs properly.

questions.question: The woman believes the new policy was motivated by

options.A: a desire to reduce the company's carbon footprint.
options.C: a need to standardise travel across departments.

questions.explanation: She thinks the finance team is trying to 'hit their quarterly targets' [3].

questions.question: What negative impact of the policy has the woman observed?

options.A: a decrease in the quality of work produced on trips
options.B: a reluctance among staff to travel for work
options.C: an increase in the number of travel-related complaints

questions.explanation: She notes people are 'delaying important trips' because they don't want to fly economy [4].

extracts.context: You hear two people discussing a new smart thermostat they’ve had installed.
extracts.audio_script: Man: I’ve just put in the 'Eco-Temp'. It’s great being able to turn the heating on from the train. || Woman: [5]I found the initial 'learning' phase to be incredibly annoying[/5], to be honest. It kept turning the heating off while I was still in the house. || Man: I had the same issue! I nearly went back to my old manual dial. || Woman: [6]I did think the energy-usage reports were very eye-opening, though[/6]. I never realized how much power we were wasting in the evenings. || Man: I agree. It definitely makes you more conscious of your habits. || Woman: It’s a clever piece of tech. I just wish it was a bit smarter at actually knowing when I’m at home.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I’ve just put in the 'Eco-Temp'. It’s great being able to turn the heating on from the train.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I found the initial 'learning' phase to be incredibly annoying, to be honest. It kept turning the heating off while I was still in the house.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I had the same issue! I nearly went back to my old manual dial.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I did think the energy-usage reports were very eye-opening, though. I never realized how much power we were wasting in the evenings.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I agree. It definitely makes you more conscious of your habits.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It’s a clever piece of tech. I just wish it was a bit smarter at actually knowing when I’m at home.

questions.question: What was the woman's main issue with the thermostat?

options.A: the high cost of the monthly subscription
options.B: the inaccuracy of the presence detection
options.C: the difficulty of the physical installation

questions.explanation: She finds it 'annoying' that it turned off the heating while she was 'still in the house' [5].

questions.question: Both speakers were impressed by the device's
questions.explanation: They both praise the 'energy-usage reports' and how it makes you 'more conscious of your habits' [6].
